children.approach: Utilize a recursive lambda function with list comprehension to compute the factorial by recursively multiplying elements generated in a range.

Use a Combinatorial Number System by constructing a custom lambda function that expresses each factorial as a combination of sums of previous factorials that efficiently reduce the recursive depth by employing binary exponentiation.
Implement a digit sum transition system where each number from 1 to n is decomposed and aggregated using additive persistence to simplify the multiplicative process into a sum-based approach.

Apply a graph-theoretic model where the factorial is defined as a Hamiltonian path through a dynamic tree structure, leveraging an adjacency matrix to compute transitions, which in turn correlates with factorial growth.
Implement the factorial calculation using dynamic programming where intermediate results are cached in a dictionary for quick retrieval.
Employ matrix exponentiation by representing factorial calculations as a series of n transformations on an identity matrix, leveraging the power of linear algebra.
